meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: Boy, 2, pulled alive from rubble six days after Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes
items.summary: A 2-year-old boy named Klieber Moran was rescued after six days trapped beneath rubble in La Guaira, Venezuela, following devastating earthquakes.

items.title: State Department congratulates Keiko Fujimori as Peru's president-elect following razor-thin vote count
items.summary: The State Department congratulates Keiko Fujimori after she was declared winner of Peru's presidential runoff by fewer than 50,000 votes out of 18 million ballots cast.

items.title: Fitness influencer says Lufthansa told her she looked ‘naked,’ forced her to zip up before boarding flight
items.summary: Fitness influencer Edda Elisa Pilz says a Lufthansa employee called her "naked" and blocked her from boarding a flight over her athletic outfit.

items.title: Thousands of police deployed across South Africa as deadly anti-immigration protests spread to multiple cities
items.summary: Thousands of police deployed across South Africa as deadly anti-immigration protests spread across multiple cities, with looting and clashes reported.

items.title: Wave of attacks on Iran's IRGC raises questions about renewed Kurdish insurgency
items.summary: Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps forces face deadly attacks across western Iran as clashes spread, raising questions about an escalating Kurdish insurgency amid U.S.–Iran talks.

items.title: Nearly 100 monkeys escape enclosure, invade neighborhoods as officials scramble to recapture them
items.summary: Nearly 100 macaques escaped a government enclosure in Lopburi, Thailand, prompting officials to use tranquilizer darts and baited cages to recapture them.

items.title: Trump admin backs Bolivia state of emergency as leftist ex-leader's loyalists fracture nation
items.summary: Bolivia's pro-US government declares a state of emergency after 50 days of blockades led by former socialist president Evo Morales and his supporters.

items.title: WATCH: Fights break out at Russian gas stations as Putin admits fuel shortages
items.summary: Putin admits Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian oil refineries are causing fuel shortages, as fights erupt at gas stations across multiple regions.

items.title: Trump's Turkey arms sale proposal sparks congressional questions before NATO summit
items.summary: The Trump administration bypasses Congress to send Turkey a $700 million arms sale including fighter jet engines, despite S-400 concerns from lawmakers.

items.title: Pope Leo pleads with breakaway Catholic group not to commit 'sin of extreme gravity'
items.summary: Pope Leo XIV issued an urgent plea to the Society of St. Pius X to halt bishop consecrations without Vatican approval, warning of excommunication.

items.title: UK asylum seekers could have to pay government $13K before applying for settlement
items.summary: Asylum seekers in Britain could have to repay more than $13,000 for accommodation before applying for settlement under proposed immigration rules.

items.title: 'Parcel bomb' explodes in Monaco residential area, leaving 2 critically injured: reports
items.summary: Surveillance video reportedly shows a man leaving a backpack moments before a blast rocked a building Monaco, critically injuring two people Monday night.

items.title: Gunman kills 6 at youth welfare facility in suspected child custody dispute: reports
items.summary: German President Frank-Walter Steinmeier said he was "deeply shocked" after six people were killed in a shooting at a facility housing mothers and children.

items.title: Families watch in horror as skydiving plane crashes in France, killing all 11 aboard
items.summary: A skydiving plane crashed shortly after takeoff in northeastern France, killing all 11 aboard. Investigators are working to determine the cause.

items.title: US military touts work to assist in Venezuela following deadly earthquakes
items.summary: The United States military is helping with disaster relief in Venezuela following the earthquakes that wreaked havoc in the South American country.

items.title: Shipping giant warns Strait of Hormuz chaos is 'new normal' as Tehran shifts 4M barrels
items.summary: Hapag-Lloyd warns of a "new normal" in the Strait of Hormuz as U.S. and Iranian strikes escalate and conflicting routing directives create chaos.

items.title: French investigators probe fatal skydiving plane crash that killed pilot and 10 parachutists
items.summary: A skydiving plane crash in northeastern France killed all 11 people aboard after the Pilatus PC-6 suffered a malfunction shortly after takeoff.

items.title: Iran targets US with psychological warfare campaign to manipulate Americans, embarrass Trump: experts
items.summary: Iran's decapitated leadership has moved its influence operations to X, using covert digital proxies to undermine Trump's nuclear deal, analysts say.

items.title: 33 rescued from Venezuelan rubble: Survival window desperately fading with nearly 50,000 missing
items.summary: Search and rescue crews race against time in La Guaira as 33 survivors are pulled from collapsed buildings while nearly 50,000 remain missing.

items.title: As America turns 250, a rare 1790 exchange between Washington and Bishop Carroll takes on new relevance
items.summary: A 1790 letter from Bishop John Carroll to George Washington sought reassurance that Catholics would be equal citizens in the new American republic.

items.title: American rescue teams pull infant alive from rubble in Venezuela days after devastating twin earthquakes
items.summary: Video shows U.S. search-and-rescue teams pulling an infant alive from rubble in Venezuela more than 72 hours after devastating twin earthquakes.

items.title: Gulf countries strongly condemn Iran's drone attack on Bahrain as rising tensions threaten MOU
items.summary: Persian Gulf countries are furious over Iran's aggression on Saturday with its drone strikes against targets in Bahrain. This is a major escalation.

items.title: Farage says mass migration has changed the UK ‘literally beyond recognition,’ believes party can win election
items.summary: Nigel Farage calls Britain's political system "completely broken" after Starmer's resignation and says Reform UK would have every chance of winning.

items.title: Venezuelan earthquake death toll hits at least 920 as US rescuers race against critical survival window
items.summary: Venezuela was hit with 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude earthquakes on Wednesday, and, in the aftermath, aid agencies are still trying to find people who are trapped.

items.title: Pilot dead, 13 injured after small plane crashes into towering skyscraper
items.summary: A small aircraft crashed into Beijing's tallest skyscraper, the 108-story CITIC Tower, prompting an information blackout from Chinese authorities.
